description:
You are working as an MLOps engineer on a small side-project that lives in the path  
/home/user/experiments.  
Inside that directory you will already find two files:

1. /home/user/experiments/VERSION  
   • Contains **exactly one line** with the current semantic version string, presently  
     1.4.2

2. /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md  
   • A Markdown changelog that follows “Keep a Changelog” & Semantic Versioning rules.  
   • Its first line is a level-1 heading “# Changelog”.  
   • Under that, each released version is documented in descending order (newest first).  
     Every released version section looks like:

        ## [<version>] - <YYYY-MM-DD>
        ### <Change-type heading>   (e.g. Added / Changed / Fixed, etc.)
        - <bullet-point change description>

   • The current topmost release section is for version 1.4.2 and is already present.

Task
-----

1. Perform a **patch version bump** from 1.4.2 ➜ **1.4.3**.  
   This means the file /home/user/experiments/VERSION must end up containing **exactly**:

        1.4.3

   (terminated by a single trailing newline, no extra spaces).

2. Add a new release entry for 1.4.3 at the very top of /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md, directly beneath “# Changelog”, following the exact format below:

        ## [1.4.3] - 2023-08-29
        ### Changed
        - Experiment tracking paths now include dataset hash.

   Important formatting constraints the automated checker will verify:

   • The line “## [1.4.3] - 2023-08-29” must begin with exactly two hash symbols, one space, an open square bracket, the version string, a close square bracket, one space, a hyphen, one space, and the date.  
   • The “### Changed” sub-heading must appear **once** directly under the version heading.  
   • The bullet line must start with a single hyphen followed by one space.  
   • There must be a single blank line (newline) **after** the bullet list (i.e. the next line after “- …hash.” is empty or is the start of the previous 1.4.2 section).  
   • The pre-existing content for version 1.4.2 and any older versions must remain unchanged and follow after the new 1.4.3 section.

3. You must accomplish both modifications with **one single terminal command** (pipelines / chained shell operators are allowed, but everything must be typed and executed in a single line before pressing Enter).

4. After running that single command, display the first 15 lines of /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md so that the result is visible in the console.

What the evaluator checks
-------------------------

• /home/user/experiments/VERSION exists and contains exactly “1.4.3” followed by a newline.  
• /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md begins with the lines:

    # Changelog
    ## [1.4.3] - 2023-08-29
    ### Changed
    - Experiment tracking paths now include dataset hash.

  and the rest of the file is unchanged after this insertion.  
• Only one command (as entered into the history) was used to accomplish the changes.  
• The command output shown in the session must include the first 15 lines of the new CHANGELOG.md.

Hints (non-prescriptive)
------------------------

• Using command substitutions, sed/awk, or a short inline shell script joined with a semicolon can help achieve the “single command” requirement.  
• Make sure your command both updates VERSION and injects the new block at the correct place in CHANGELOG.md before printing the file preview.
truth:
Initial setup before the task starts
------------------------------------

A. Directory layout:
   /home/user/experiments/
     ├── VERSION
     └── CHANGELOG.md

B. File contents:

1. /home/user/experiments/VERSION  
   (single line, LF-terminated)
   ----------------------------
   1.4.2

2. /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md  
   ------------------------------------
   # Changelog
   ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-22
   ### Fixed
   - Resolved metrics logging race condition.

   ## [1.4.1] - 2023-08-15
   ### Added
   - Introduced early stopping callback.

   (…possibly more historic entries continue)

Expected state **after** successful task execution
--------------------------------------------------

1. /home/user/experiments/VERSION  
   (single line, LF-terminated)
   ----------------------------
   1.4.3

2. /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md  
   (the top 11 lines will now be)
   ------------------------------------
   # Changelog
   ## [1.4.3] - 2023-08-29
   ### Changed
   - Experiment tracking paths now include dataset hash.

   ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-22
   ### Fixed
   - Resolved metrics logging race condition.

   (the remainder of the previous content remains byte-for-byte identical)

The test harness will:
• Read /home/user/experiments/VERSION and expect exactly “1.4.3\n”.  
• Grep the first 5 non-empty lines of /home/user/experiments/CHANGELOG.md and compare them to the expected block above.  
• Optionally diff the rest of the file to ensure only the insertion point changed.  
• Verify that the executed history (from “history”) shows only one command between prompt-returns prior to the “cat/head” preview.
